rulings.Case Title: JAMES BRECHT et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSCECUTE
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Goodrich Corporation¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is granted. No opposition filed. A hearing is required unless counsel for Goodrich informs the plaintiffs of the tentative ruling and the plaintiffs tell defense counsel that they do not wish to contest it. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOE EARL DAVIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Fluor Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Fluor was dismissed on May 25. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES AIKINS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder By Cleaver-Brooks, Inc. To Defendant John Crane, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss In Favor Of Pending Federal Court Action
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. DEFENDANT CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC.'s Joinder To Defendant John Crane Inc.'s Motion to stay of dismiss. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s motion to stay or dismiss in favor of the pending federal court action and joinders are denied. A stay or dismissal on forum non-conveniens grounds cannot be granted because the federal district court does not have jurisdiction over all defendants. The defendants in federal court are not necessary parties to this case. Assuming without deciding that the court has authority to stay this action on grounds of comity, the court declines to do so because this is a preference case, there are more defendants in this action than in the federal case, and there does not appear to be any prejudice to any of the defendants by this case proceeding to trial before the federal action proceeds to trial. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY CAMPBELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Slakey Brothers, Inc.S Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.310 And 583.360
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Slakey Brothers, Inc.'s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.310 And 583.360is granted. No opposition filed.
